name: The Security State Bank
description_short: Community-focused FDIC-insured bank operating since 1920 with checking, savings, loans, and insurance services across South Dakota locations.
description_long:
The Security State Bank was established in 1920 as The Security National Bank of Emery, South Dakota, following approval from the Comptroller of the Currency. The bank survived the Great Depression and subsequent closures of competing regional banks, eventually expanding to multiple locations including Alexandria (1940) and Canistota (1983). Today it operates as a full-service community bank with the explicit mission of "putting the customer 1st."

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of deposit products including checking accounts, savings accounts, and Certificates of Deposit. On the lending side, they provide consumer loans, auto loans, mortgages, and other credit products. Deposit holders receive debit cards and access to credit cards. Convenience features include two ATM cash dispensing terminals, online banking with mobile app access (available on Apple App Store and Google Play), and the option to enroll in internet banking. The bank also houses the J.L. Arend Insurance Agency on-premises.

What distinguishes The Security State Bank is its long community history and survival through major economic crises. The bank has maintained local ownership and decision-making rather than consolidating into larger regional or national networks. Their emphasis on small-town community banking since 1920 differentiates them from larger institutions, and their integration of insurance services provides some additional convenience for existing customers.

The bank serves customers in rural South Dakota communities with traditional banking services. However, as a small regional institution, they likely offer fewer digital innovations and specialized products compared to larger banks. Customers in markets far from their physical locations (Emery, Alexandria, or Canistota) would need to conduct most banking remotely or may be better served by national institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC insured deposits backed by U.S. Government
Mobile banking app available on both iOS and Android platforms
Two ATM locations for cash access
Integrated J.L. Arend Insurance Agency for one-stop financial services
Free Shazam Brella fraud monitoring app for debit cardholders
Over 100 years of operational history and local community presence since 1920
Online banking enrollment with password recovery options
cons: Limited to three physical locations in South Dakota (Emery, Alexandria, Canistota), restricting branch access for most customers
No information provided on interest rates, fees, loan terms, or product competitiveness compared to other banks
Small institution may offer fewer specialized products or investment options than larger regional/national banks
best_for: Residents of rural South Dakota communities (Emery, Alexandria, Canistota area) seeking local banking relationships
Customers prioritizing community bank values and personal service over digital innovation
Small business owners and farmers in the region with traditional banking needs
diagnosis: The Security State Bank is best for rural South Dakota residents in or near Emery, Alexandria, or Canistota who value long-established community banking and local decision-making. The main caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside these communities would need to conduct banking remotely or find a more conveniently located institution.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
